63	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
64	
65	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
66	
67	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
68	
69	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
70	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
71	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
72	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
73	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
74	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
75	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
76	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
77	
78	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
79	map of `kToSection`. -/
80	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
81	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
82	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
83	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
84	
85	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
86	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
87	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
88	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
89	  rfl
90	
91	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
92	natural in `U`. -/
93	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
94	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
95	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
96	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
97	  rfl
98	
99	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
100	`algebraMap`. -/
101	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
102	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
103	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
104	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
105	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
106	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
107	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
108	
109	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
110	
111	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
112	
113	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
114	
115	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
116	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
117	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
118	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
119	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
120	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
121	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
122	      map_add' := fun x y => by
123	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
124	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
125	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
126	        congr 1
127	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
128	          (C := C) f r }
129	  map_id U := by
130	    ext x
131	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
132	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
133	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
134	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
135	    ext x
136	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
137	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
138	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
139	
140	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
141	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
142	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
143	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
144	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
145	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
146	  rfl
147	
148	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
149	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
150	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
151	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
152	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
153	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
154	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
155	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
156	
157	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
158	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
159	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
160	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
161	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
162	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
163	
164	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
165	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
166	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
167	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
168	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
169	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
170	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
171	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
173	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
174	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
175	  Iso.refl _
176	
177	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
178	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
179	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
180	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
181	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
182	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
183	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
184	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
185	noncomputable abbrev HModule
186	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
187	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
188	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
189	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
190	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
191	
192	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
193	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
194	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
195	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
196	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
197	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
198	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
199	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
200	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
201	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
202	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
203	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
204	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
205	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
206	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
207	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
208	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
209	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
210	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
211	
212	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
213	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
214	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
215	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
216	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
217	
218	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
219	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
220	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
221	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
222	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
223	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
224	
225	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
226	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
227	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
228	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
229	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
230	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
231	iter-015+. -/
232	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
233	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
234	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
235	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
236	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
237	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
238	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
239	
240	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
241	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
242	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
243	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
244	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
245	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
246	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
247	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
248	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
249	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
250	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
251	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
252	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
253	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
254	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
255	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
256	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
257	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
258	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
259	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
260	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
261	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
262	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
263	
264	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
265	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
266	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
267	`X ↦ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
268	i.e.\ to a presheaf `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
269	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
270	
271	The codomain is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` (not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
272	`Abelian.extFunctor n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
273	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
274	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
275	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
276	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
277	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
278	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
279	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
280	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
281	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
282	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
283	    (n : ℕ) :
284	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
285	  Functor.flip
286	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
287	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
288	
289	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
290	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
291	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
292	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
293	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
294	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
295	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
296	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
297	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
298	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
299	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
300	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
301	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
302	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
303	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
304	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
305	
306	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
307	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
308	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
309	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
310	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
311	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for
312	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf → HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
313	
314	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (...).obj (op S.X₃)` is the biproduct in
315	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
316	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
317	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
318	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
319	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `δ`, iter-019 the LES sequence
320	and exactness theorem. -/
321	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
322	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
323	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
324	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
325	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
326	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
327	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
328	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
329	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
330	  biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
331	              ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
332	
333	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
334	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
335	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
336	as a single map out of the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
337	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file
338	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L67–70) for
339	the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
340	
341	The negation `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` uses the
342	preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms; the sign is the standard
343	Mayer-Vietoris convention encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech
344	complex. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). -/
345	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
346	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
347	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
348	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
349	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
350	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
351	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
352	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
353	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) :=
354	  biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
355	              (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
356	
357	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-018): the third Mayer-Vietoris LES building
358	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the composition-is-zero lemma asserting
359	`HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0`. Direct mirror
360	of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod_fromBiprod`
361	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`,
362	L72–75) for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
363	
364	The proof unwinds via the biproduct universal property
365	(`biprod.lift_desc : biprod.lift a b ≫ biprod.desc c d = a ≫ c + b ≫ d`),
366	the preadditive negation (`Preadditive.comp_neg`), the addition-of-negation
367	arithmetic (`← sub_eq_add_neg`), the zero-iff-equal arithmetic
368	(`sub_eq_zero`), the contravariant functoriality of `cohomologyPresheaf`
369	(`← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`), and the Mayer-Vietoris square
370	factorisation `S.toSquare.fac` (which says `S.f₁₂ ≫ S.f₂₄ = S.f₁₃ ≫ S.f₃₄`).
371	Probe-confirmed proof (iter-018 plan-agent); the `simp only` set transfers
372	verbatim from Mathlib's `AddCommGrpCat`-flavored proof because every step
373	is value-category-agnostic.
374	
375	The `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` attribute generates the post-composition
376	form `… ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod … ≫ Z = 0 ≫ Z = 0` and registers the lemma
377	as a `simp` lemma; both are required for downstream Mayer-Vietoris LES
378	exactness arguments. -/
379	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
380	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod
381	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
382	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
383	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
384	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
385	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
386	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0 := by
387	  simp only [HModule'_toBiprod, HModule'_fromBiprod, biprod.lift_desc,
388	    Preadditive.comp_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_zero,
389	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, S.toSquare.fac]
390	
391	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): `ModuleCat.free k`
392	is left adjoint to `forget (ModuleCat k)`. Mathlib's
393	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` provides the adjunction
394	`ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)` but does not register
395	the corresponding `IsLeftAdjoint` instance (whereas `AddCommGrpCat.free.IsLeftAdjoint`
396	is registered in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` L84). This
397	project-local instance fills the gap so that `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
398	acquires the necessary `PreservesColimit` instance, used by
399	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` below. -/
400	instance ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint
401	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint :=
402	  ⟨_, ⟨ModuleCat.adj k⟩⟩
403	
404	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
405	pushout-of-free-sheaves analog of Mathlib's
406	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf`
407	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L154–160).
408	States that the image of the Mayer-Vietoris square `S` under the composite
409	`yoneda ⋙ Functor.whiskeringRight ⋅ (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J _`
410	is a pushout square in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. The proof transfers the
411	type-level pushout `S.isPushout` through `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
412	(which preserves pushouts because `(ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint`, registered
413	in `ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint` above) and adjusts via the canonical iso
414	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso`.
415	
416	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); the proof is verbatim from Mathlib L156–160
417	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. Used downstream in iter-020+ to
418	prove `Mono` / `Epi` / `Exact` instances on the iter-019 short complex
419	`HModule'_shortComplex`, and ultimately in the iter-022+ `δ` connecting hom. -/
420	lemma HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf
421	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
422	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
423	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
424	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
425	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
426	      presheafToSheaf J _)).IsPushout :=
427	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
428	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
429	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
430	        (S.map yoneda))
431	
432	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
433	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
434	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
435	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234–249) for the
436	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
437	
438	The objects are the sheafified free `ModuleCat k`-valued presheaves on the four
439	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X₂` and `S.X₃`
440	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f₁₂)
441	– (yoneda.map S.f₁₃)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum
442	`(yoneda.map S.f₂₄) + (yoneda.map S.f₃₄)` desced through the biproduct).
443	The `zero` proof (i.e.\ `f ≫ g = 0`) follows from the
444	`cokernelCofork.condition` of the pushout square in
445	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` above.
446	
447	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); structure-literal mirror of Mathlib L235–249
448	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The `@[simps]` attribute generates
449	field-projection simp lemmas (`HModule'_shortComplex_X₁`, `..._X₂`, `..._X₃`,
450	`..._f`, `..._g`) consumed by the iter-020+ `Mono`/`Epi`/`Exact`/`ShortExact`
451	proofs and the iter-022+ `δ` definition. -/
452	@[simps]
453	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
454	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
455	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
456	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
457	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
458	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
459	  X₁ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₁ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
460	  X₂ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₂ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k) ⊞
461	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₃ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
462	  X₃ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₄ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
463	  f :=
464	    biprod.lift
465	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₂) _))
466	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₃) _))
467	  g :=
468	    biprod.desc
469	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₂₄) _))
470	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₃₄) _))
471	  zero :=
472	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
473	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition
474	
475	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): the free-module
476	functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` preserves monomorphisms.
477	Mathlib registers `AddCommGrpCat.instPreservesMonomorphismsFree :
478	AddCommGrpCat.free.PreservesMonomorphisms` in
479	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` but does not register the
480	corresponding instance for `ModuleCat.free k` in
481	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean`. This project-local
482	instance fills the gap so that, after `simp only [biprod.lift_snd]`, the
483	typeclass-search engine can discharge the residual `Mono` goal in the proof
484	of `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` below.
485	
486	The proof: for an injective function `f : X → Y` between types,
487	`(ModuleCat.free k).map f = ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.lmapDomain k k f)` (defeq),
488	and `Finsupp.mapDomain f` is injective on `Finsupp X k → Finsupp Y k` by
489	Mathlib's `Finsupp.mapDomain_injective`. Bridging via `ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective`
490	recovers `Mono` in `ModuleCat k`. -/
491	instance ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms
492	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).PreservesMonomorphisms := by
493	  refine ⟨fun {X Y} f hf ↦ ?_⟩
494	  have hf' : Function.Injective f := (CategoryTheory.mono_iff_injective f).mp hf
495	  rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
496	  exact Finsupp.mapDomain_injective hf'
497	
498	-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` is a
499	-- monomorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
500	-- `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L251–257 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
501	-- The `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` attribute is
502	-- required because the typeclass-search engine needs to unfold the `dsimp`-normal
503	-- form of `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` past structure-literal projection.
504	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
505	instance HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono
506	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
507	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
508	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
509	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
510	    Mono (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f := by
511	  have : Mono ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).f ≫ biprod.snd) := by
512	    dsimp
513	    simp only [biprod.lift_snd]
514	    infer_instance
515	  exact mono_of_mono _ biprod.snd
516	
517	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).g` is an
518	epimorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
519	`MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L259–261 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
520	The proof is a one-line term-mode body using
521	`ShortComplex.exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
522	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
523	instance HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi
524	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
525	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
526	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
527	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
528	    Epi (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g :=
529	  ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel.2
530	    ⟨(HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork⟩).2
531	
532	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact`,
533	i.e. the kernel of `g` equals the image of `f` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`.
534	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L263–265 with
535	`AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line term-mode
536	body using `ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
537	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
538	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_exact
539	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
540	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
541	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
542	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
543	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact :=
544	  ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel _
545	    (HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork
546	
547	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact`,
548	the short-exact predicate (combining `Mono f`, `Epi g`, and `Exact`) in
549	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean`
550	L267–268 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line
551	anonymous-constructor: the `Mono f` and `Epi g` predicates are typeclass-resolved
552	from `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` and `HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi`, leaving
553	only the `exact` field which is filled with `HModule'_shortComplex_exact`. The
554	named lemma is consumed in iter-021+ as `S.HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact.extClass`
555	to define the connecting hom `HModule'_δ` of the Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
556	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact
557	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
558	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
559	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
560	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
561	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact where
562	  exact := HModule'_shortComplex_exact k S

564	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-021): the connecting homomorphism `δ` of the
565	Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology.
566	For a Mayer-Vietoris square `S` and adjacent cohomological degrees `n₀ + 1 = n₁`,
567	the morphism
568	  δ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (op S.X₁) ⟶
569	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (op S.X₄)
570	in `AddCommGrpCat` is the precomposition with the extension class
571	`(HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass : Ext (...X₁...) (...X₃...) 1`
572	followed by `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` to wrap the resulting `AddMonoidHom` as a
573	categorical morphism. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.δ`
574	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L112–114)
575	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
576	
577	The `[HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` typeclass is required (the
578	`Ext.precomp` operation depends on it) and matches the corresponding
579	requirement on iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `..._cohomologyPresheaf`,
580	iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `..._fromBiprod`, and iter-018
581	`HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod`.
582	
583	This connecting hom is the missing third link of the Mayer-Vietoris exact
584	sequence: combined with iter-017's `toBiprod` (sum of restriction) and
585	`fromBiprod` (difference of restriction), iter-018's `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0`,
586	and the iter-022+ packaging into a `ComposableArrows`-form sequence + iter-023+
587	sequence-iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + iter-024+ exactness theorem, it
588	will yield the full LES `... → Hⁿ(X₄) → Hⁿ(X₂) ⊞ Hⁿ(X₃) → Hⁿ(X₁) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X₄) → ...`
589	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology. -/
590	noncomputable def HModule'_δ
591	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
592	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
593	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
594	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
595	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
596	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
597	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
598	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
599	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
600	
601	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-022): the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
602	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology, packaged as a `ComposableArrows` of
603	length 5 in `AddCommGrpCat`. The five composable morphisms are, in order,
604	`HModule'_toBiprod` at degree `n₀`, `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₀`, the
605	connecting hom `HModule'_δ` from degree `n₀` to degree `n₁`, `HModule'_toBiprod`
606	at degree `n₁`, and `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₁`. Direct mirror of
607	Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.sequence`
608	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L120–122)
609	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
610	
611	The `noncomputable abbrev` form is load-bearing: downstream `dsimp`-based
612	unfolding (in iter-023+ `HModule'_sequenceIso` and iter-024+
613	`HModule'_sequence_exact`) needs to access `mk₅`'s field-projection simp
614	lemmas through the abbrev. -/
615	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_sequence
616	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
617	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
618	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
619	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
620	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
621	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
622	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
623	  ComposableArrows.mk₅ (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₀) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀)
624	    (HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h)
625	    (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₁)

627	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 1: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_toBiprod`
628	(mirror Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L48–64). -/
629	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_apply
630	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
631	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
632	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
633	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
634	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
635	    {n : ℕ} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
636	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
637	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
638	        ⟨(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
639	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
640	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
641	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
642	  ext
643	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
644	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
645	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
646	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
647	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
648	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]

651	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77–83). -/
652	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
653	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
654	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
655	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
656	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
657	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
658	    {n : ℕ}
659	    (y₁ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂))

665	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]

670	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism

679	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]

692	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

694	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
695	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
696	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
697	    {n : ℕ}
698	    (x : ↑((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
699	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃))) :

708	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
709	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]

712	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
713	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124–138). The technical heart
714	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
715	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
716	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
717	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
718	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
719	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
720	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
721	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
722	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
723	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
724	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
725	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
726	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
727	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans

740	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023

743	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
744	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
745	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
746	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
747	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
748	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
749	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
750	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
751	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
752	
753	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
754	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]

756	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
757	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
758	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
759	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
760	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
761	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
762	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
763	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
764	
765	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
766	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
767	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
768	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
769	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
770	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
771	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
772	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
773	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
774	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
775	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1

781	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
782	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
783	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
784	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
785	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
786	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
787	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
788	
789	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
790	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
791	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
792	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
793	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
794	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
795	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
796	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
797	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
798	
799	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
800	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
801	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
802	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
803	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
804	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
805	acyclic. -/
806	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
807	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
808	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
809	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
810	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
811	
812	end AlgebraicGeometry

405	
406	\begin{remark}[Why this completes the Mayer-Vietoris algebraic prerequisites]
407	  Mathlib's Mayer-Vietoris LES for an abelian sheaf in \lstinline{AddCommGrpCat}-flavor uses both $\Sheaf.\HPrime\,n\,X$ (the cohomology-of-an-open carrier) \emph{and} the $H^0$ identification with global sections (the spliced-in zeroth term). On the $\ModuleCat\,k$ side, the iter-014 $\HModulePrime$ provides the carrier and the iter-015 \lstinline{HModule'_zero_linearEquiv} provides the $H^0$ identification: together they are the algebraic preliminaries. The substantive Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem itself (mirroring \lstinline{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean} line-by-line for the $\ModuleCat\,k$-flavored carriers) is queued for iter-016+.
408	\end{remark}
409	
410	\section{Functorial wrappers: cohomology of an open as a presheaf (iter-016)}
411	\label{sec:hmodule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
412	
413	After Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime} and Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_zero_linearEquiv} the cohomology-of-an-open carrier $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ is well-defined together with its degree-zero $H^0$ identification with a Hom group. The next natural step is the \emph{functoriality of the carrier in the open variable $X$}: assembling $X \mapsto \HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ into a presheaf
414	\[
415	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n \;\colon\; \Cop \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
416	\]
417	together with the bifunctorial form (varying $F$ as well)
418	\[
419	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;\colon\; \Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k) \;\Longrightarrow\; (\Cop \;\Longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat).

1090	    \item iter-026: exactness theorem $\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{sequence\_exact}$ + $\delta$-zero $\mathtt{simp}$ companions $\HModulePrime\_\delta\_\toBiprod$ + $\HModulePrime\_\fromBiprod\_\delta$ (this section).
1091	  \end{itemize}
1092	
1093	  Iter-027 starts a fresh cycle: split this file along the natural semantic boundary (the iter-016--iter-026 MV-LES infrastructure moves to a new file $\mathtt{Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean}$), then iter-028+ begins the Serre-finiteness work specialised to a $2$-affine cover of a proper $k$-curve --- the multi-iteration theorem $\Module.\Finite\,k\,(\HModule\,k\,F\,i)$ for $F = \toModuleKSheaf\,C$, which $\mathtt{smoothOfRelativeDimension\_genus}$ (one of the four \texttt{Jacobian.lean} instances) will eventually consume.
1094	
1095	  Iter-026 sorry-count trajectory: $9 \to 12 \to 9$ (three new \lstinline{sorry} sites in the three lemmas; all filled in the iter-026 prover round).
1096	\end{remark}
1097	
1098	\section{Use in the project}
1099	
1100	The composition of the four typeclass instances of Sections~\ref{sec:moduleK_prereqs}–\ref{sec:moduleK_ext}, Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_toModuleKSheaf}, and Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule} delivers Phase~A step~5 in full and bridges to Phase~A step~6:
1101	
1102	\begin{itemize}
1103	  \item For $C : \Over\,(\Spec\,(\CommRingCat\,k))$, the $\ModuleCat\,k$-flavoured cohomology
1104	    \[
1105	      H^n_{\Module\,k}(C, \struct C) \;:=\; \mathtt{HModule}\,k\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)\,n
1106	    \]
1107	    is well-defined and automatically carries a $\Module\,k$ instance, so $\Module.\mathtt{finrank}\,k\,(\HModule\,k\,(\toModuleKSheaf\,C)\,1) : \mathbb N$ is well-defined.
1108	  \item The forgetful functor $\ModuleCat\,k \to \AddCommGrpCat$ recovers the iter-004 abelian-group cohomology (Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare}), so the new $\HModule$ commutes with the existing $\Sheaf.\mathtt H$ on the underlying type level.
1109	  \item Phase~A step~6 (Serre finiteness, $\finrank_k H^i(C, \mathcal F) < \infty$) remains the deep work to come, but is now expressible inside Mathlib's standard $\Module\,k$ API: the statement of Serre finiteness becomes a $\Module.\mathtt{Finite}\,k\,(\HModule\,k\,F\,n)$ predicate.
1110	\end{itemize}
1111	
1112	\section{Mathlib gap}
1113	
1114	After Section~\ref{sec:scheme_toModuleKSheaf} is implemented, this chapter has no remaining Mathlib gap. The construction reuses only the following established Mathlib pieces:
1115	
1116	\begin{itemize}
1117	  \item \lstinline{CategoryTheory.HasSheafify}, \lstinline{CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard}, and the abelian/$\Linear\,k$ enrichment on $\Sheaf(J, \ModuleCat\,k)$ (verified by \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probes; underlies Sections~\ref{sec:moduleK_prereqs}–\ref{sec:moduleK_ext}).
1118	  \item \lstinline{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.\Gamma SpecIso}, \lstinline{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.app}, \lstinline{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.presheaf} (existing Mathlib API for the structure morphism and the structure-sheaf restriction maps).
1119	  \item \lstinline{Algebra.compHom} / \lstinline{RingHom.toAlgebra} (algebra-from-ring-map).
1120	  \item \lstinline{CategoryTheory.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget} (concrete-category sheaf criterion; transfers the sheaf condition between $\CommRingCat$ and $\ModuleCat\,k$).
1121	\end{itemize}
1122	
1123	Nothing genuinely new is required from Mathlib. The iter-006 plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe verifies the full $\sim$80-LOC construction end-to-end against Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}.

128	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): `ModuleCat.free k`
129	is left adjoint to `forget (ModuleCat k)`. Mathlib's
130	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` provides the adjunction
131	`ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)` but does not register
132	the corresponding `IsLeftAdjoint` instance (whereas `AddCommGrpCat.free.IsLeftAdjoint`
133	is registered in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` L84). This
134	project-local instance fills the gap so that `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
135	acquires the necessary `PreservesColimit` instance, used by
136	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` below. -/
137	instance ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint
138	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint :=
139	  ⟨_, ⟨ModuleCat.adj k⟩⟩

141	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
142	pushout-of-free-sheaves analog of Mathlib's
143	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf`
144	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L154–160).
145	States that the image of the Mayer-Vietoris square `S` under the composite
146	`yoneda ⋙ Functor.whiskeringRight ⋅ (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J _`
147	is a pushout square in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. The proof transfers the
148	type-level pushout `S.isPushout` through `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
149	(which preserves pushouts because `(ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint`, registered
150	in `ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint` above) and adjusts via the canonical iso
151	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso`.
152	
153	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); the proof is verbatim from Mathlib L156–160
154	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. Used downstream in iter-020+ to
155	prove `Mono` / `Epi` / `Exact` instances on the iter-019 short complex
156	`HModule'_shortComplex`, and ultimately in the iter-022+ `δ` connecting hom. -/
157	lemma HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf
158	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
159	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
160	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
161	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
162	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
163	      presheafToSheaf J _)).IsPushout :=
164	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
165	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
166	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
167	        (S.map yoneda))
168	
169	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
170	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
171	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
172	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234–249) for the
173	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
174	
175	The objects are the sheafified free `ModuleCat k`-valued presheaves on the four
176	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X₂` and `S.X₃`
177	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f₁₂)
178	– (yoneda.map S.f₁₃)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum
179	`(yoneda.map S.f₂₄) + (yoneda.map S.f₃₄)` desced through the biproduct).
180	The `zero` proof (i.e.\ `f ≫ g = 0`) follows from the
181	`cokernelCofork.condition` of the pushout square in
182	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` above.
183	
184	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); structure-literal mirror of Mathlib L235–249
185	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The `@[simps]` attribute generates
186	field-projection simp lemmas (`HModule'_shortComplex_X₁`, `..._X₂`, `..._X₃`,
187	`..._f`, `..._g`) consumed by the iter-020+ `Mono`/`Epi`/`Exact`/`ShortExact`
188	proofs and the iter-022+ `δ` definition. -/
189	@[simps]
190	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
191	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
192	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
193	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
194	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
195	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
196	  X₁ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₁ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
197	  X₂ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₂ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k) ⊞
198	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₃ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
199	  X₃ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₄ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
200	  f :=
201	    biprod.lift
202	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₂) _))
203	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₃) _))
204	  g :=
205	    biprod.desc
206	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₂₄) _))
207	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₃₄) _))
208	  zero :=
209	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
210	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition
211	
212	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): the free-module
213	functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` preserves monomorphisms.
214	Mathlib registers `AddCommGrpCat.instPreservesMonomorphismsFree :
215	AddCommGrpCat.free.PreservesMonomorphisms` in
216	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` but does not register the
217	corresponding instance for `ModuleCat.free k` in
218	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean`. This project-local
219	instance fills the gap so that, after `simp only [biprod.lift_snd]`, the
220	typeclass-search engine can discharge the residual `Mono` goal in the proof
221	of `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` below.
222	
223	The proof: for an injective function `f : X → Y` between types,
224	`(ModuleCat.free k).map f = ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.lmapDomain k k f)` (defeq),
225	and `Finsupp.mapDomain f` is injective on `Finsupp X k → Finsupp Y k` by
226	Mathlib's `Finsupp.mapDomain_injective`. Bridging via `ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective`
227	recovers `Mono` in `ModuleCat k`. -/
228	instance ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms
229	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).PreservesMonomorphisms := by
230	  refine ⟨fun {X Y} f hf ↦ ?_⟩
231	  have hf' : Function.Injective f := (CategoryTheory.mono_iff_injective f).mp hf
232	  rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
233	  exact Finsupp.mapDomain_injective hf'
234	
235	-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` is a
236	-- monomorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
237	-- `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L251–257 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
238	-- The `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` attribute is
239	-- required because the typeclass-search engine needs to unfold the `dsimp`-normal
240	-- form of `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` past structure-literal projection.
241	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
242	instance HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono
243	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
244	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
245	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
246	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
247	    Mono (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f := by
248	  have : Mono ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).f ≫ biprod.snd) := by
249	    dsimp
250	    simp only [biprod.lift_snd]
251	    infer_instance
252	  exact mono_of_mono _ biprod.snd
253	
254	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).g` is an
255	epimorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
256	`MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L259–261 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
257	The proof is a one-line term-mode body using
258	`ShortComplex.exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
259	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
260	instance HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi
261	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
262	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
263	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
264	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
265	    Epi (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g :=
266	  ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel.2
267	    ⟨(HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork⟩).2
268	
269	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact`,
270	i.e. the kernel of `g` equals the image of `f` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`.
271	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L263–265 with
272	`AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line term-mode
273	body using `ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
274	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
275	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_exact
276	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
277	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
278	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
279	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
280	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact :=
281	  ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel _
282	    (HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork
283	
284	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact`,
285	the short-exact predicate (combining `Mono f`, `Epi g`, and `Exact`) in
286	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean`
287	L267–268 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line
288	anonymous-constructor: the `Mono f` and `Epi g` predicates are typeclass-resolved
289	from `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` and `HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi`, leaving
290	only the `exact` field which is filled with `HModule'_shortComplex_exact`. The
291	named lemma is consumed in iter-021+ as `S.HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact.extClass`
292	to define the connecting hom `HModule'_δ` of the Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
293	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact
294	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
295	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
296	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
297	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
298	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact where
299	  exact := HModule'_shortComplex_exact k S
300	
301	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-021): the connecting homomorphism `δ` of the
302	Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology.
303	For a Mayer-Vietoris square `S` and adjacent cohomological degrees `n₀ + 1 = n₁`,
304	the morphism
305	  δ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (op S.X₁) ⟶
306	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (op S.X₄)
307	in `AddCommGrpCat` is the precomposition with the extension class
308	`(HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass : Ext (...X₁...) (...X₃...) 1`
309	followed by `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` to wrap the resulting `AddMonoidHom` as a
310	categorical morphism. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.δ`
311	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L112–114)
312	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
313	
314	The `[HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` typeclass is required (the
315	`Ext.precomp` operation depends on it) and matches the corresponding
316	requirement on iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `..._cohomologyPresheaf`,
317	iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `..._fromBiprod`, and iter-018
318	`HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod`.
319	
320	This connecting hom is the missing third link of the Mayer-Vietoris exact
321	sequence: combined with iter-017's `toBiprod` (sum of restriction) and
322	`fromBiprod` (difference of restriction), iter-018's `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0`,
323	and the iter-022+ packaging into a `ComposableArrows`-form sequence + iter-023+
324	sequence-iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + iter-024+ exactness theorem, it
325	will yield the full LES `... → Hⁿ(X₄) → Hⁿ(X₂) ⊞ Hⁿ(X₃) → Hⁿ(X₁) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X₄) → ...`
326	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology. -/
327	noncomputable def HModule'_δ
328	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
329	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
330	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
331	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
332	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
333	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
334	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
335	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
336	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
337	
338	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-022): the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
339	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology, packaged as a `ComposableArrows` of
340	length 5 in `AddCommGrpCat`. The five composable morphisms are, in order,
341	`HModule'_toBiprod` at degree `n₀`, `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₀`, the
342	connecting hom `HModule'_δ` from degree `n₀` to degree `n₁`, `HModule'_toBiprod`
343	at degree `n₁`, and `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₁`. Direct mirror of
344	Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.sequence`
345	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L120–122)
346	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
347	
348	The `noncomputable abbrev` form is load-bearing: downstream `dsimp`-based
349	unfolding (in iter-023+ `HModule'_sequenceIso` and iter-024+
350	`HModule'_sequence_exact`) needs to access `mk₅`'s field-projection simp
351	lemmas through the abbrev. -/
352	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_sequence
353	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
354	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
355	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
356	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
357	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
358	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
359	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
360	  ComposableArrows.mk₅ (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₀) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀)
361	    (HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h)
362	    (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₁)
363	
364	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 1: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_toBiprod`
365	(mirror Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L48–64). -/
366	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_apply
367	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
368	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
369	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
370	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
371	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
372	    {n : ℕ} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
373	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
374	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
375	        ⟨(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
376	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
377	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
378	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
379	  ext
380	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
381	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
382	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
383	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
384	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
385	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
386	
387	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 2: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_fromBiprod`
388	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77–83). -/
389	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
390	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
391	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
392	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
393	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
394	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
395	    {n : ℕ}
396	    (y₁ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂))
397	    (y₂ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃)) :
398	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n

401	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op y₂ := by
402	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]
403	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
404	  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]

407	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism

416	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
417	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))

424	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 4: bridges the same machinery for the `fromBiprod`

427	attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
428	lemma HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
429	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
430	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
431	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
432	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
433	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
434	    {n : ℕ}
435	    (x : ↑((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
436	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃))) :

445	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
446	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
447	  cat_disch

449	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
450	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124–138). The technical heart
451	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
452	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
453	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
454	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
455	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
456	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
457	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
458	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
459	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
460	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
461	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
462	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
463	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
464	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
465	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
466	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
467	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
468	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
469	    (Iso.refl _)
470	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
471	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
472	    (by dsimp; rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]; rfl)
473	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
474	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
475	
476	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
477	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
478	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
479	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
480	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
481	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
482	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
483	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
484	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
485	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
486	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
487	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
488	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
489	
490	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
491	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
492	lemma HModule'_δ_toBiprod
493	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
494	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
495	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
496	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
497	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
498	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
499	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
500	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
501	
502	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
503	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
504	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
505	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
506	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
507	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
508	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
509	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
510	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
511	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
512	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1
513

/-
